speaker: CORNWALL
text: Post speedily to my lord your

speaker: CORNWALL
text: husband. Show him this letter.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: The army of France is landed.—Seek out

speaker: CORNWALL
text: the traitor Gloucester.

speaker: REGAN
text: Hang him instantly.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Pluck out his eyes.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Leave him to my displeasure.—Edmund,

speaker: CORNWALL
text: keep you our sister company. The revenges we are

speaker: CORNWALL
text: bound to take upon your traitorous father are not

speaker: CORNWALL
text: fit for your beholding. Advise the Duke, where you

speaker: CORNWALL
text: are going, to a most festinate preparation; we are

speaker: CORNWALL
text: bound to the like. Our posts shall be swift and

speaker: CORNWALL
text: intelligent betwixt us.—Farewell, dear sister.—

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Farewell, my lord of Gloucester.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: How now? Where’s the King?

speaker: OSWALD
text: My lord of Gloucester hath conveyed him hence.

speaker: OSWALD
text: Some five- or six-and-thirty of his knights,

speaker: OSWALD
text: Hot questrists after him, met him at gate,

speaker: OSWALD
text: Who, with some other of the lord’s dependents,

speaker: OSWALD
text: Are gone with him toward Dover, where they boast

speaker: OSWALD
text: To have well-armèd friends.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Get horses for your mistress.

speaker: GONERIL
text: Farewell, sweet lord, and sister.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Edmund, farewell.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Go seek the traitor Gloucester.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Pinion him like a thief; bring him before us.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Though well we may not pass upon his life

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Without the form of justice, yet our power

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Shall do a court’sy to our wrath, which men

speaker: CORNWALL
text: May blame but not control.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Who’s there? The traitor?

speaker: REGAN
text: Ingrateful fox! ’Tis he.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Bind fast his corky arms.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: What means your Graces? Good my friends, consider

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You are my guests; do me no foul play, friends.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Bind him, I say.

speaker: REGAN
text: Hard, hard. O filthy traitor!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Unmerciful lady as you are, I’m none.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: To this chair bind him.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Villain, thou shalt find—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: By the kind gods, ’tis most ignobly done

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To pluck me by the beard.

speaker: REGAN
text: So white, and such a traitor?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Naughty lady,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: These hairs which thou dost ravish from my chin

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Will quicken and accuse thee. I am your host;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: With robber’s hands my hospitable favors

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: You should not ruffle thus. What will you do?

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Come, sir, what letters had you late from France?

speaker: REGAN
text: Be simple-answered, for we know the truth.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: And what confederacy have you with the traitors

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Late footed in the kingdom?

speaker: REGAN
text: To whose hands

speaker: REGAN
text: You have sent the lunatic king. Speak.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I have a letter guessingly set down

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Which came from one that’s of a neutral heart,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And not from one opposed.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Cunning.

speaker: REGAN
text: And false.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Where hast thou sent the King?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To Dover.

speaker: REGAN
text: Wherefore to Dover? Wast thou not charged at peril—

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Wherefore to Dover? Let him answer that.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: I am tied to th’ stake, and I must stand the course.

speaker: REGAN
text: Wherefore to Dover?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Because I would not see thy cruel nails

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Pluck out his poor old eyes, nor thy fierce sister

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In his anointed flesh stick boarish fangs.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The sea, with such a storm as his bare head

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: In hell-black night endured, would have buoyed up

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: And quenched the stellèd fires;

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Yet, poor old heart, he holp the heavens to rain.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: If wolves had at thy gate howled that stern time,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Thou shouldst have said “Good porter, turn the key.”

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: All cruels else subscribe. But I shall see

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: The wingèd vengeance overtake such children.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: See ’t shalt thou never.—Fellows, hold the chair.—

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Upon these eyes of thine I’ll set my foot.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: He that will think to live till he be old,

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Give me some help!

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O cruel! O you gods!

speaker: REGAN
text: One side will mock another. Th’ other too.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: If you see vengeance—

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Hold your hand, my lord.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: I have served you ever since I was a child,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: But better service have I never done you

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Than now to bid you hold.

speaker: REGAN
text: How now, you dog?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: If you did wear a beard upon your chin,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: I’d shake it on this quarrel. What do you mean?

speaker: CORNWALL
text: My villain?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: Nay, then, come on, and take the chance of anger.

speaker: REGAN
text: Give me thy sword. A peasant stand up thus?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: O, I am slain! My lord, you have one eye left

speaker:
FIRST
               
              SERVANT
text: To see some mischief on him. O!

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Lest it see more, prevent it. Out, vile jelly!

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Where is thy luster now?

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: All dark and comfortless! Where’s my son Edmund?—

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Edmund, enkindle all the sparks of nature

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: To quit this horrid act.

speaker: REGAN
text: Out, treacherous villain!

speaker: REGAN
text: Thou call’st on him that hates thee. It was he

speaker: REGAN
text: That made the overture of thy treasons to us,

speaker: REGAN
text: Who is too good to pity thee.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: O my follies! Then Edgar was abused.

speaker: GLOUCESTER
text: Kind gods, forgive me that, and prosper him.

speaker: REGAN
text: Go thrust him out at gates, and let him smell

speaker: REGAN
text: His way to Dover.

speaker: REGAN
text: How is ’t, my lord? How look you?

speaker: CORNWALL
text: I have received a hurt. Follow me, lady.—

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Turn out that eyeless villain. Throw this slave

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Upon the dunghill.—Regan, I bleed apace.

speaker: CORNWALL
text: Untimely comes this hurt. Give me your arm.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: I’ll never care what wickedness I do

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: If this man come to good.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: If she live long

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: And in the end meet the old course of death,

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Women will all turn monsters.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Let’s follow the old earl and get the Bedlam

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: To lead him where he would. His roguish madness

speaker:
SECOND
               
              SERVANT
text: Allows itself to anything.

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: Go thou. I’ll fetch some flax and whites of eggs

speaker:
THIRD
               
              SERVANT
text: To apply to his bleeding face. Now heaven help him!
